# Liveblocks Comments with @Mentions Resolver

## Background
You are working on a Next.js 14 (App Router) collaboration app that uses Liveblocks for real-time comments. A scaffold is already in place: it boots a Next.js server, wraps the page with `LiveblocksProvider` + `RoomProvider`, and renders a `<Composer />` plus a `<Thread />` list inside a room. The scaffold ALSO already authenticates users through `Liveblocks.identifyUser` in `/api/liveblocks-auth` (so the current user is identified via an ID token), and exposes an in-memory user directory through `/api/users`.

What is missing is `@mentions` support: the `<Composer />` currently does NOT show any mention suggestions when the user types `@`, and submitted threads render raw user IDs instead of friendly names.

Your job is to wire mentions end-to-end by implementing the `resolveMentionSuggestions` and `resolveUsers` callbacks on `LiveblocksProvider`, backed by the existing `/api/users` directory.

## Requirements
- Read `run-id` from the `ZEALT_RUN_ID` environment variable. Use it as the room ID suffix (the scaffold already does this via `NEXT_PUBLIC_ROOM_ID_BASE`/`ZEALT_RUN_ID`, do not change that contract).
- Implement `resolveMentionSuggestions({ text })` on `<LiveblocksProvider />`. It must:
  - Fetch the user directory from the local `/api/users` endpoint.
  - Return user IDs (string[]) whose `name` or `id` matches the typed `text` as a case-insensitive prefix.
  - When `text` is an empty string, return all available user IDs.
- Implement `resolveUsers({ userIds })` on `<LiveblocksProvider />`. It must:
  - Fetch from `/api/users`.
  - Return an array of `{ name, avatar }` objects in the SAME ORDER as the input `userIds`.
  - For unknown IDs, return `undefined` at that position (do not throw).
- Keep the existing `<Composer />` and `<Thread />` ready-made components from `@liveblocks/react-ui`. Do not replace them with custom primitives.
- The mention chip rendered in a submitted comment MUST display the user's resolved `name` (e.g. `Alice`), NOT the raw user ID (e.g. `user-alice`).
- Do NOT mock the Liveblocks client. The app must connect to real Liveblocks cloud using the `LIVEBLOCKS_SECRET_KEY` and `NEXT_PUBLIC_LIVEBLOCKS_PUBLIC_KEY` environment variables already provided.

## User Directory (already served by `/api/users`)
The scaffold exposes a hardcoded in-memory list of 5 users at `GET /api/users`:

```json
[
  { "id": "user-alice",   "name": "Alice",   "avatar": "https://liveblocks.io/avatars/avatar-1.png" },
  { "id": "user-bob",     "name": "Bob",     "avatar": "https://liveblocks.io/avatars/avatar-2.png" },
  { "id": "user-charlie", "name": "Charlie", "avatar": "https://liveblocks.io/avatars/avatar-3.png" },
  { "id": "user-dave",    "name": "Dave",    "avatar": "https://liveblocks.io/avatars/avatar-4.png" },
  { "id": "user-eve",     "name": "Eve",     "avatar": "https://liveblocks.io/avatars/avatar-5.png" }
]
```

The auth endpoint at `/api/liveblocks-auth` already calls `liveblocks.identifyUser({ userId: "user-alice", groupIds: [] }, { userInfo: { name, avatar } })`, so the currently authenticated user is `user-alice`.

## Implementation Hints
- The mention resolver callbacks belong on `<LiveblocksProvider />` (see Liveblocks React docs: `resolveMentionSuggestions`, `resolveUsers`). They are passed as props and are called automatically by the `<Composer />`, `<Thread />` and mention primitives.
- `resolveMentionSuggestions` receives `{ text, roomId }` and must return a `string[]` of user IDs (synchronously or via `Promise`).
- `resolveUsers` receives `{ userIds }` and must return an array of user-info objects (or `undefined`s) in the same order as the input.
- Filter both by case-insensitive prefix on the `name` field; fall back to matching on the `id` if needed (so `@al` matches Alice).
- Use the global `fetch` available in the browser to call `/api/users` from inside the resolver. You do NOT need to add any new API route.
- The Liveblocks `<Composer />` already wires up the mention UI; you do not have to render the suggestion dropdown yourself.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Project path: `/home/user/myproject`
- Start command: `npm run dev`
- Port: `3000`
- Routes:
  - `GET /` renders the comments page. The page must contain an editable `<Composer />` (a `[contenteditable]` editor that accepts text input) and, after a comment is submitted, a `<Thread />` rendering the comment.
  - `GET /api/users` returns the JSON array of 5 hardcoded users described above (already implemented; must keep working).
  - `POST /api/liveblocks-auth` returns a Liveblocks ID token for `user-alice` (already implemented; must keep working).
- Mention behavior (the part you implement):
  - When the user types `@` followed by characters that prefix-match a user's `name` (case-insensitive), the Liveblocks mention suggestion dropdown MUST appear with one or more matching users (their resolved `name`s must be visible in the dropdown).
  - Selecting a suggestion MUST insert a mention chip into the composer.
  - After submitting the comment, the rendered thread MUST display the mentioned user's `name` (NOT the raw `user-<id>` string).
- Do not change `package.json` start scripts or the port. The dev server must be reachable at `http://localhost:3000` after running the start command.
Let me start by exploring the project structure to understand what's already in place.

input.result:
The `@mentions` support has been wired end-to-end in `/home/user/myproject/app/Room.tsx`. Here's what was implemented:

### Changes made — `app/Room.tsx`

Two module-level async functions were added and passed as props to `<LiveblocksProvider>`:

**`resolveMentionSuggestions({ text, roomId })`**
- Fetches the user directory from `/api/users`
- When `text` is empty, returns all user IDs
- Otherwise filters users whose `name` **or** `id` starts with `text` (case-insensitive prefix match)
- Returns a `string[]` of matching user IDs — consumed by `<Composer>` to populate the suggestion dropdown

**`resolveUsers({ userIds })`**
- Fetches the user directory from `/api/users`
- Builds a `Map<id, user>` for O(1) lookup
- Returns `{ name, avatar }` objects in the **same order** as the input `userIds`
- Returns `undefined` at positions where a user ID is not found (no throws)
- Consumed by `<Thread>` to render friendly names instead of raw IDs

### Behavior
- Typing `@` in the `<Composer>` shows all 5 users in the dropdown
- Typing `@al` narrows to "Alice"; `@b` to "Bob", etc.
- Selecting a suggestion inserts a mention chip
- After submitting, the rendered `<Thread>` shows `@Alice` (or whichever user), not `@user-alice`

The dev server is running at `http://localhost:3000` with zero TypeScript errors.
